channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: AWS nabs white hot gen AI media creation startup fal, becoming its preferred cloud provider
item.description: Generative AI’s rapid transition from text-based chatbots to high-fidelity media—spanning images, video, spatial 3D, and audio—has exposed a glaring bottleneck in the modern tech stack: infrastructure. Rendering pixels in real-time requires a staggering amount of compute, and developers are increasingly struggling to manage fragmented GPU clusters just to keep their applications online.Enter fal, a generative media creation platform that has quietly become the connective tissue for 2.5 million developers across the globe, offering literally hundreds of leading AI image, video, and audio creati...

item.title: New study finds most Americans think the pace of AI development is moving too fast — and they also don't believe everyone will truly benefit from it
item.description: Most Americans are worried about the rapid pace of AI innovation – are regulators struggling to keep up now?

item.title: Wear OS 7 will keep track of deliveries and sports scores on your wrist
item.description: Amid the flurry of today's Google I/O announcements, Google shared details about Wear OS 7, the next major update to its smartwatch platform. To help you keep track of things like deliveries and sports scores, Wear OS 7 will get the iPhone-style Live Updates that were introduced on Android last year - which can appear […]

item.title: Plex is tripling the price of a lifetime pass to $750 after doubling it last year
item.description: I am dying to know how much money Plex is about to make the next six weeks charging people to stream their own video from their own homes. Today, it's giving every prospective customer until July 1st to lock in a lifetime subscription at today's rates - before it triples the price to $750. Plex […]

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Wednesday, May 20 (game #1074)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Wednesday, May 20 (game #1577)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Wednesday, May 20 (game #808)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: Amazon could be upgrading its next generation of Kindles with replaceable batteries for users — but is it enough to make up for ending support for its older but widely-used e-readers?
item.description: Amazon could be upgrading its next lineup of Kindles with replaceable batteries, making it easier for users to repair their own devices.

item.title: Literary Prizewinners Are Facing AI Allegations. It Feels Like the New Normal
item.description: Three of five regional winners of the prestigious Commonwealth Short Story Prize are suspected of relying on chatbots. They’re certainly not alone.

item.title: Android XR is finally starting to feel real
item.description: Google has a lot to prove, but its smart glasses will have a few big advantages.

item.title: 'The people that built the spacecraft are not alive anymore': NASA admits Voyager spacecraft code is still being maintained — even if barely anyone alive knows how to use the language it's programmed in
item.description: NASA’s Voyager spacecraft still operates using ancient assembly code while the engineers who understood their systems gradually disappear forever.

item.title: Discord now has end-to-end encryption on all calls
item.description: It's a win for privacy on the social app.

item.title: All the Gemini announcements from Google I/O 2026
item.description: Google had a slew of Gemini announcements to share at I/O 2026. Here are the big ones.

item.title: California’s Wildfire Season Is Already Overactive
item.description: Major fires are threatening homes and ecologically sensitive areas following a hot, dry winter.

item.title: Demis Hassabis said this might be the ‘foothills of the singularity.’ What?
item.description: Welcome to a "profound moment for humanity," according to Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis, who closed out Google I/O's keynote presentation on Tuesday, saying: Google's cutting-edge research and products will help unlock AGI's incredible potential for the benefit of the entire world. When we look back at this time, I think we will realize that […]...
